messages.content: hi
Hello! How can I assist you today?

Please note that we have checked with the HK Inland Revenue Department (“IRD”) and have been informed that your 2022/23 HK Individual Tax return has not yet been issued as of today. We will maintain regular communication with the IRD on your behalf. As soon as the tax return is issued, we will arrange to prepare and finalize it for your review and signature. 

please check grammar

The grammar in the provided statement is correct. However, to improve clarity, I would suggest revising the first sentence to say: "Please note that according to the HK Inland Revenue Department ("IRD"), the 2022/23 HK Individual Tax return has not yet been issued as of today, as we have confirmed through communication with the department."

Please note that according to the HK Inland Revenue Department (“IRD”), your 2022/23 HK Individual Tax Return has not yet been issued as of today, as we have confirmed through communication with the IRD. We will maintain regular communication with the IRD on your behalf. As soon as the tax return is issued, we will arrange to prepare and finalize it for your review and signature.
The revised statement looks correct to me with no grammatical errors.

Meanwhile, we understand that you have completed the GA About Me on our portal. We note that you have input that your spouse had income in Hong Kong (less than HK$132,000) between April 1, 2022 to March 31, 2023 (i.e. year of assessment 2022/23). Please confirm:- 

(1)	If your spouse had earned income chargeable to Hong Kong salaries tax for the year of assessment 2022/23
(2)	If so, please provide your spouse’s 2022/23 employer’s return (Form IR56B) for our reference

1. Whether your spouse had earned income that is chargeable to Hong Kong salaries tax for the year of assessment 2022/23.

Meanwhile, We have noticed that you have completed the GA About Me form on our portal, and you have indicated that your spouse had income in Hong Kong (less than HK$132,000) during the year of assessment 2022/23 (i.e. from April 1, 2022 to March 31, 2023). Please confirm the following:

(1)	Whether your spouse had earned income that is chargeable to Hong Kong salaries tax for the year of assessment 2022/23
(2)	If so, please provide your spouse’s 2022/23 employer’s return (Form IR56B) for our reference
The grammar and punctuation in the revised statement you have provided is correct. It is clear and concise, and conveys the message effectively.

Apologize for not getting to you earlier. 

Please be advised that Hong Kong adopts a territorial basis tax system. If your income is arising in and derived from Hong Kong, you will be chargeable to Hong Kong salaries tax. We understand that you are on short term assignment to HK from March 2023, and as you are performing services in HK during your STA, you will be subject to HK salaries tax. 

Please also note that HK tax year runs from 1 April to 31 March next year. As such, you are required to file HK salaries tax for the year 2022/23 (i.e. from 1 April 2022 to 31 March 2023). In order for us to prepare your 2022/23 HK tax filings, please complete the GA About Me (an online data collector) for our further handling. 

With regards to the GA calendar you mentioned, please note that it is a tool that to keep track of your travel records, including dates of entry and exit from Hong Kong, as well as your business trips to other locations, etc.. As such, please complete and submit the GA calendar as well. 


check grammar

The grammar in the provided statement is correct. However, to improve clarity, I would suggest revising the second sentence to say: "We understand that you will be on a short term assignment to HK starting from March 2023, and as you will be performing services in HK during your assignment, you will be subject to HK salaries tax."

Apologize for not getting to you earlier. 

Please be advised that Hong Kong adopts a territorial basis tax system. If your employment income is arising in and derived from Hong Kong, you will be chargeable to Hong Kong salaries tax. We understand that you are on short term assignment to HK from March 22, 2023, and as you are performing services in HK during your STA, you will be subject to HK salaries tax. 

Please also note that HK tax year runs from 1 April to 31 March next year. As such, you are required to file HK salaries tax filings for the year 2022/23 (i.e. from 1 April 2022 to 31 March 2023). In order for us to prepare your 2022/23 HK tax filings, please complete the GA About Me (an online data collector) for our further handling. 

With regards to the GA calendar, please note that it is a tool that to keep track of your travel records, including dates of entry and exit from Hong Kong, as well as your personal/business trips to other locations, etc.. As such, please complete and submit the GA calendar as well.

As HK tax assessment year runs from 1 April to 31 March next year, and the assignees in the previous list have departed from HK during the tax year of 2021/22 (i.e. before March 2022). Thus, they were authorised for the assistance for the tax year 2021/22. They should have the bonus in 2022 unless they left HSBC. 

In addition, we would like to further confirm another 3 assignees who were authorised for this tax year of 2022/23. We understand that those 3 assignees were not include the PY2022 bonus records too.  We would appreciate it if you could help us to check the records. 


check the above sentences

The grammar in the provided statement is not completely correct. Here's a possible revision:

"As the Hong Kong tax assessment year runs from 1 April to 31 March of the following year, the assignees listed previously had departed from Hong Kong during the 2021/22 tax year (i.e., before March 2022) and were authorized for assistance for that tax year. They should receive their bonuses in 2022 unless they have left HSBC. 

Furthermore, we would like to confirm another three assignees who were authorized for the 2022/23 tax year but were not included in the PY2022 bonus records. We would appreciate it if you could check the records for us." 

The revised statement uses correct punctuation and clarifies the information to make it easier to understand.

As the Hong Kong tax assessment year runs from 1 April to 31 March of the following year, the assignees listed previously had departed from Hong Kong during the 2021/22 tax year (i.e., before March 2022) and were authorized for assistance for that tax year. They should have received their bonuses, please check and provide this information for our further preparation. 

Furthermore, we would like to confirm another three assignees who were authorized for the 2022/23 tax year but were not included in the PY2022 bonus records. We would appreciate it if you could also check the records for us. Thank you in advance for your help.

Thanks for the below. 

As yesterday was a HK public holiday, we will need more time to review/amend the necessary information. We will get back to you as soon as possible earlier next week. 


check grammar

The grammar in the provided statement is correct. However, to improve clarity, I would suggest adding the word "by" after "as soon as possible" for more clarity. Here's a possible revision:

"Due to the Hong Kong public holiday yesterday, we will need more time to review/amend the necessary information. We will get back to you as soon as possible by earlier next week."

Thanks for the below. 

Due to the Hong Kong public holiday yesterday, we will need more time to review/amend the necessary information. We will get back to you as soon as possible by earlier next week. Please let us know if there are any concerns.

The grammar in the provided statement is correct. However, I would suggest revising the wording slightly for more clarity. Here is a possible amended version:

"Due to the Hong Kong public holiday yesterday, we will require additional time to review/amend the necessary information. We will get back to you with an update by early next week. Please let us know if you have any concerns." 

In this revision, I changed "as soon as possible" to "with an update" for more specific clarity as to what will be provided. I also changed "earlier" to "early" to more accurately convey the intended meaning.
is it polite enough to say "Please let us know if you have any concerns."

Yes, the phrase "Please let us know if you have any concerns" is polite and appropriate. It shows that you are open to hearing feedback or addressing any questions or issues that the recipient may have.

Thanks for your reply. Please kindly provide us the housing details once they are available. 

Further to your comments, we attach the updated briefing checklist for your record. Please provide us a scanned copy of signed checklist or acknowledge the contents of the checklist by email within 5 working days for our processing requirements. If we do not receive your email by 5 working days of the email, we assume that you acknowledge the contents of the attached checklist.

check grammar

The grammar in the provided statement is correct. However, it can be improved for clarity and conciseness. Here is a possible revision:

"Thank you for your reply. Please provide us with the housing details once they become available.

Regarding your comments, we have attached an updated briefing checklist for your records. Please sign the checklist and send us a scanned copy, or acknowledge the contents within five working days by email, for our processing requirements. If we do not receive your email within five working days, we will assume that you acknowledge the contents of the checklist." 

In this revision, I have changed the wording to make it clearer and more concise. I replaced "once they are available" with "once they become available," which is a more natural phrase. I rephrased the second sentence to clarify that the company needs either a signed copy of the checklist or an email acknowledging its contents. Finally, I adjusted the wording slightly to make it simpler and clearer.

Meanwhile, please also note that the GA Organizer (About Me) and GA Calendar are tools to collect your personal information / travel record via our GA portal for onward preparation of your Individual Tax Return, the GA Portal site address is gade.com and your username is the primary email address which we hold for you. Please keep track on your travel records on the GA Calendar. We will release the GA Organizer (About Me) in around May 2024 to collect the necessary information to prepare your 2023/24 Individual Tax Return.

The grammar in the provided statement is correct. However, I would suggest revising it slightly for clarity. Here's a possible revision:

"Please note that the GA Organizer (About Me) and GA Calendar are tools available on our GA portal, gade.com, that help us prepare your Individual Tax Return by collecting your personal information and travel records. Your username is the primary email address that we hold for you. Please keep track of your travel records on the GA Calendar. We will release the GA Organizer (About Me) in approximately May 2024 to collect the necessary information for your 2023/24 Individual Tax Return." 

In this revision, I streamlined the language to make it simpler and clearer. I also rearranged the sentences to make the information flow more logically and added some details regarding the GA portal.

Please note that the HK Inland Revenue Department has sent your HK Individual Tax Return (“Return”) for the year 2022/23 to us. Since we are not authorized by the Bank to handle your 2022/23 HK salaries tax matters, we will redirect the Return to the below address for your own handling. Please inform us immediately if you prefer another address to receive your Return.

"Please be advised that the HK Inland Revenue Department has sent your HK Individual Tax Return (“Return”) for the year 2022/23 to our office. However, as we are not authorized by HSBC to handle your 2022/23 HK salaries tax matters, we will redirect the Return to the address below for your personal handling. If you would prefer another address to receive your Return, please inform us immediately." 

In this revision, I clarified that the Return has been sent to the company's office, not to the client directly, and that the company will redirect it to the client's address. I also explicitly mentioned that the company is not authorized by HSBC to handle the client's tax matters, which may help to avoid any confusion.
Please also note that we have applied extension until August 3, 2023 to file the 2022/23 Individual Tax Return.
